stories.headline: US Stocks Soar as Trump Says He’s Called Off New Iran Strikes
stories.summary: US stocks rose to session highs following two straight losing days, as President Donald Trump said he’s called off planned military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the three-month war.

stories.headline: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
stories.summary: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

stories.headline: Trump says he is cancelling fresh strikes on Iran and hints at possible peace deal
stories.summary: Trump’s social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the Middle East closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

stories.headline: Drone strikes on central Sudanese city kill up to 23: NGO
stories.summary: Local rights group Emergency Lawyers blamed the attack on the RSF, which did not immediately claim responsibility.

stories.headline: Trump calls off Iran strikes planned for Thursday night
stories.summary: US president claims progress in negotiations for new deal with Tehran

stories.headline: India protests to US after three sailors killed in tanker attack
stories.summary: New Delhi calls for ‘de-escalation’ in Gulf but appears to have little appetite for confrontation with Washington

stories.headline: Trump and Hegseth Broadcast U.S. Military Strikes Before They Happen
stories.summary: Commanders do not usually speak publicly about future operations to avoid jeopardizing the mission’s success.

stories.headline: Trump says US strikes canceled after threat to ‘take’ Iran’s Kharg Island
stories.summary: After saying US would hit Iran 'very hard', Trump signals possible breakthrough in ceasefire negotiations.

stories.headline: Property deeds no protection for Palestinians as settler violence spreads
stories.summary: Israeli settlers follow Palestinian Bedouins across the West Bank, chasing them from their land.

stories.headline: World chess body suspends Russia over activities in occupied-Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia is accused of usurping control of the game in areas of Ukraine captured by Russian forces since 2022.

stories.headline: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
stories.summary: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injure

stories.headline: Russia 'lost standing’ despite ‘a breather’ from higher oil prices, IMF chief says
stories.summary: While Russia has emerged as one of the very few winners from the recent disruptions to the global oil market amid the Iran war, this is not enough to offset the hit of sanctions and isolation, the managing director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) told Euronews.

stories.headline: IMF warns against further relaxation of euro area fiscal rules
stories.summary: The IMF has urged euro area countries to tighten their fiscal policies rather than relaxing them further in response to the energy crisis, with an extra adjustment effort required from high-debt nations.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
stories.summary: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

stories.headline: ECB raises interest rates for first time since 2023
stories.summary: Central bank is first in G7 to increase borrowing costs in response to Middle East energy crisis

stories.headline: UK defence secretary resigns over spending plan
stories.summary: John Healey says Keir Starmer ‘unable’ and Treasury ‘unwilling’ to provide essential budget

stories.headline: Novo’s Wegovy Pill Wins Approval in UK for Obesity Treatment
stories.summary: Novo Nordisk A/S’s Wegovy pill was approved in the UK, opening up the obesity market to people who are wary of taking a shot.

stories.headline: Wall Street Looks for Proof of AI Profit
stories.summary: Franklin Templeton Senior Investment Strategist Katrina Dudley joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why Wall Street is no longer rewarding AI spending without proof of profits. She compares SpaceX's massive investments to the railroad boom, and argues that AI is enhancing worker productivity rather than eliminating jobs. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Vance Boelter Pleads Guilty in Assassination of Minnesota Lawmaker
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors said they would not seek the death penalty for Vance Boelter, who still faces charges in state court.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Pushes Limits of Election Investigations
stories.summary: The Justice Department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.

stories.headline: We can’t deliver ‘like-for-like-services’ for people kicked off the NDIS, states warn Albanese government

stories.headline: Aukus is among Australia’s worst foreign policy decisions and requires ‘heroic’ optimism, Gareth Evans says
stories.summary: Former Labor foreign affairs minister says belief US would defend Australia in event of an existential attack is a ‘ludicrous delusion’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAukus will prove to be one of the worst defence and foreign policy decisions ever made by an Australian government and is only being permitted by Donald Trump in order to destroy Chinese nuclear threats

stories.headline: At least 22 killed as Pakistani military helicopter crashes in Kashmir
stories.summary: Officials launch inquiry after 'technical fault' causes an army Mi-17 to crash in Pakistan-administered Kashmir.

stories.headline: Embattled Palestinian president of Oxford Union: ‘I’m not resigning’
stories.summary: Arwa Elrayess, the first Palestinian to lead University of Oxford's debating society, has been troubled by falsehoods.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Brussels to scrap 'rebates' for some member states – or Italy will demand them too
stories.summary: The Italian Prime Minister has attacked the EU's disbursement to so-called frugal states and warned Brussels that Rome's patience with them is at an end.

stories.headline: French singer Patrick Bruel under formal investigation for rape
stories.summary: Bruel was released from custody under judicial supervision while an examining magistrate looks more deeply into the case.

stories.headline: Thousands march in Pakistan-administered Kashmir as clashes kill at least 15
stories.summary: An uneasy silence has blanketed the capital, with empty streets, closed shops and patrolling police.

stories.headline: John Healey: the ex-trade unionist who privately battled the Treasury
stories.summary: Previously talked of as a potential caretaker prime minister, his efforts as defence secretary could not generate the spending increase the military wanted

stories.headline: New York Penn Station Rehab Eyes Billions in Federal Funding
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Kaske joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The entities transforming New York’s Penn Station are slated to learn how much federal money will be directed to the project in about six months, according to Peter Cipriano, CEO of the Halmar-Skanska consortium, the renovation’s master developer. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Emerging Assets Rebound as Trump Cancels Strike, Signals Deal
stories.summary: Emerging-market currencies jumped and stocks erased earlier losses after President Donald Trump canceled planned attacks on Iran, fueling optimism that a deal was imminent.

stories.headline: Wall Street Races to Turn SpaceX Into a Leveraged Retail Trade
stories.summary: The $15 trillion ETF industry isn’t waiting long to see how SpaceX performs before turning it into a leveraged trading vehicle.

stories.headline: Oaktree Sees Distressed Opportunities as Kick-the-Can Era Ends
stories.summary: Higher-for-longer interest rates means over-leveraged companies are running out of ways to repay their borrowings ahead of a looming maturity wall, according to Brook Hinchman, a managing director at Oaktree Capital Management LP.

stories.headline: Citigroup Seeks $5 Billion From Year’s First High-Grade Bonds
stories.summary: Citigroup Inc. is seeking to raise about $5 billion from its first investment-grade bond sale of 2026, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: European Stocks Snap Losing Run; ECB Hike Lifts Banks
stories.summary: European equities snapped a four-day losing streak, with energy shares leading gains in the index while the European Central Bank’s first interest-rate hike in three years lifted banks.

stories.headline: SpaceX IPO Could Test Market, Says 1789 Capital’s Abrahimzadeh
stories.summary: The public debut of SpaceX is poised to become a major test of investor appetite and market infrastructure, according to 1789 Capital partner Paul Abrahimzadeh, whose firm counts the company as its largest portfolio holding.

stories.headline: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with their children
stories.summary: Budget airline describes inquiry as ‘bogus’ as watchdog says it is only large carrier flying from UK to impose chargeBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s biggest low-cost airline, Ryanair, is facing an investigation over the mandatory fee it charges a parent to sit with their child.The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said the Irish carrier’s terms and conditions require at least one parent to sit with their children, including those wit

stories.headline: Iran shuts Hormuz strait: But wasn’t it already closed?
stories.summary: The strait is a crucial maritime route, through which 20 percent of global oil and natural gas is shipped in peacetime.

stories.headline: Japan central bank deputy Uchida in spotlight while chief hospitalized
India doubles public investment in 5 years to build rails, chip plants
Indonesia's shrinkflation belies government's 'strong' fundamentals claim
Chinese firefighting equipment makers target untapped EU market
Singapore minister makes case for globalization in age of anxiety
Rare protest over animal abuse spotlights China's 'mass-movement' fears
Uncertainty plagues Taiwan's drone industry after budget cuts
Fragile countries 'want to look up' to China, US: East-Timor president
Australia roiled by mounting scandal at KPMG
Tokyo Disneyland 'magic' in doubt as operator's stock falls
Cyprus proposes €32.8bn cut to next EU budget as compromise between rival camps
stories.summary: Cyprus proposes a 2% cut — worth €32.8bn — to the EU's draft €2 trillion budget for 2028–2034, in a bid to bridge the gap between "frugals" and cohesion-minded member states. The figures, now on the table for the first time, will serve as the starting point for the sensitive negotiations ahead.

stories.headline: Gold Jumps After Trump Reverses Stance on Iran Military Strikes
stories.summary: Gold jumped the most in a month after US President Donald Trump said he canceled planned military strikes against Iran, fueling hopes for a diplomatic way out of the war that has rattled global markets.

stories.headline: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
stories.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

stories.headline: Oil Tumbles as Trump Cancels Iran Strikes, Says Deal Is Close
stories.summary: Oil plunged to the lowest since April after US President Donald Trump said that he called off an imminent military escalation against Iran and signaled that a peace deal is not far off, fueling bets for an end to the war that’s disurpted global energy shipments.

stories.headline: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North SumatraExtreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival.The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans (Pongo tapanuliensis) were kil

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.headline: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
stories.summary: India's government said 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

stories.headline: Stocks Climb as Trump Signals US-Iran Deal Is Near: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Wall Street powered a rebound in stocks as oil fell after President Donald Trump canceled planned strikes against Iran and signaled a deal is near, fueling hopes for an end to the war.

stories.headline: Bill Gates says Epstein wanted personal relationship, but he 'never reciprocated'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder spent hours talking to lawmakers behind closed doors, where he also said Epstein used his marital infidelities to pressure him.

stories.headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.headline: 'I will never get over watching my home burn': Belfast residents reel after night of unrest
stories.summary: A night of disorder in Belfast saw people forced to flee their homes, in protests that followed a knife attack in the north of the city.

stories.headline: Growing backlash in Japan over Trump's use of anime characters
stories.summary: The US president has angered some fans by using the images of characters like Pikachu and Naruto.

stories.headline: Nancy Mace’s thrashing in South Carolina governor’s race caps a rough downfall
stories.summary: The congresswoman finished fifth in the state’s GOP gubernatorial primary, according to unofficial returns, solidly losing even her own home county and district.

stories.headline: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
stories.summary: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global WitnessLeading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims.The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democrati

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanese city of Tyre despite Iranian warning to stop attacks
stories.summary: Iran warned Israel on Monday that it could resume hostilities if attacks on its Lebanese ally Hezbollah do not stop.

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
stories.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.headline: Murder of Lyhanna, 11, enrages France and turns up heat on government
stories.summary: Protesters are angry that the suspect had already been reported to police last August in a separate case.

stories.headline: World's largest chipmaker does not rule out price rises as costs increase
stories.summary: In a rare interview, a senior executive at TSMC discusses the AI boom, the geopolitics of chips and what it means for the price of electronics.

stories.headline: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
stories.summary: Kyiv has made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to the territories it occupies.

stories.headline: How one of India's most successful female politicians is losing her party
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee's once-dominant Trinamool Congress party is unravelling, weeks after losing power in West Bengal.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang on trip to revitalise China-North Korea ties
stories.summary: Kim Jong-un welcomes Chinese leader on visit to renew relations strained amid Pyongyang’s closeness with RussiaXi Jinping has arrived in North Korea for a two-day trip, his first in nearly seven years, as China’s leader looks to revitalise ties with his junior ally.Footage published by China’s Xinhua state news agency showed an Air China plane carrying Xi and his wife, Peng Liyuan, touching down at Pyongyang’s Sunan international airport. Continu

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
stories.summary: Teachers demanding higher pay have been demonstrating for several days and threaten to disrupt the tournament if their demands are not met.
